$3, $4, $5, $6)\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n                &next_version,\n                &schema_json,\n                &created_at_ns,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn get_schema(&self, collection: &CollectionId) -> Result<Option<CollectionSchema>, AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT schema_json FROM schemas\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\n                 ORDER BY version DESC\n                 LIMIT 1\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        match rows.first() {\n            Some(row) => {\n                let schema_json: serde_json::Value = row.get(\"schema_json\");\n                let schema: CollectionSchema = serde_json::from_value(schema_json)?;\n                Ok(Some(schema))\n            }\n            None => Ok(None),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn get_schema_version(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n        version: u32,\n    ) -> Result<Option<CollectionSchema>, AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT schema_json FROM schemas\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3 AND version = $4\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n                &(version as i32),\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        match rows.first() {\n            Some(row) => {\n                let schema_json: serde_json::Value = row.get(\"schema_json\");\n                let schema: CollectionSchema = serde_json::from_value(schema_json)?;\n                Ok(Some(schema))\n            }\n            None => Ok(None),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn list_schema_versions(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<(u32, u64)>, AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT version, created_at_ns FROM schemas\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\n                 ORDER BY version ASC\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        Ok(rows\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|row| {\n                (\n                    row.get::<_, i32>(\"version\") as u32,\n                    row.get::<_, i64>(\"created_at_ns\") as u64,\n                )\n            })\n            .collect())\n    }\n\n    fn delete_schema(&mut self, collection: &CollectionId) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM schemas\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn put_collection_view(&mut self, view: &CollectionView) -> Result<CollectionView, AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(&view.collection)?;\n        if !self.collection_exists_in_namespace(&key.collection, &key.namespace)? {\n            return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n                \"collection '{}' is not registered\",\n                view.collection.as_str()\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let current_version = self\n            .block(self.client.query_opt(\n                \"SELECT version FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n                &[\n                    &key.collection.as_str(),\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?\n            .map_or(0, |row| row.get::<_, i32>(\"version\"));\n        let next_version = current_version + 1;\n\n        let updated_at_ns = SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_nanos() as i64;\n\n        let mut versioned = view.clone();\n        versioned.collection = key.collection.clone();\n        versioned.version = next_version as u32;\n        versioned.updated_at_ns = Some(updated_at_ns as u64);\n        let view_json = serde_json::to_value(&versioned)?;\n\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO collection_views\n                    (collection, database_name, schema_name, version, view_json, updated_at_ns, updated_by)\n                 VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7)\n                 ON CONFLICT (database_name, schema_name, collection) DO UPDATE SET\n                     version = EXCLUDED.version,\n                     view_json = EXCLUDED.view_json,\n                     updated_at_ns = EXCLUDED.updated_at_ns,\n                     updated_by = EXCLUDED.updated_by\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n                &next_version,\n                &view_json,\n                &updated_at_ns,\n                &versioned.updated_by,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(versioned)\n    }\n\n    fn get_collection_view(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n    ) -> Result<Option<CollectionView>, AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT view_json FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        match rows.first() {\n            Some(row) => {\n                let view_json: serde_json::Value = row.get(\"view_json\");\n                let view: CollectionView = serde_json::from_value(view_json)?;\n                Ok(Some(view))\n            }\n            None => Ok(None),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn delete_collection_view(&mut self, collection: &CollectionId) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn register_collection_in_namespace(\n        &mut self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n        namespace: &Namespace,\n    ) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        if !self.namespace_exists(namespace)? {\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\"namespace '{namespace}'\")));\n        }\n\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO collections (name, database_name, schema_name)\n                 VALUES ($1, $2, $3)\n                 ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING\",\n            &[&collection.as_str(), &namespace.database, &namespace.schema],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn unregister_collection(&mut self, collection: &CollectionId) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        let raw_collection = collection.as_str();\n        let default_namespace = Namespace::default_ns();\n        // Upgraded databases may still have a pre-fix collection_views table\n        // without the collection -> collections foreign key, and may still\n        // carry metadata rows keyed by the original qualified identifier in\n        // either the resolved namespace or the old default/default namespace,\n        // so clean them up explicitly instead of relying solely on ON DELETE\n        // CASCADE.\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM collection_views\n                     WHERE collection = $1\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\",\n                &[\n                    &raw_collection,\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                    &default_namespace.database,\n                    &default_namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?;\n        }\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM schemas\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM schemas\n                     WHERE collection = $1\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\",\n                &[\n                    &raw_collection,\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                    &default_namespace.database,\n                    &default_namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?;\n        }\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM collections\n                 WHERE name = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM collections\n                     WHERE name = $1\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\",\n                &[\n                    &raw_collection,\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                    &default_namespace.database,\n                    &default_namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?;\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn list_collections(&self) -> Result<Vec<CollectionId>, AxonError> {\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT name FROM collections\n                 ORDER BY database_name ASC, schema_name ASC, name ASC\",\n            &[],\n        ))?;\n\n        Ok(rows\n            .iter()\n            .map(|row| {\n                let name: String = row.get(\"name\");\n                CollectionId::new(name)\n            })\n            .collect())\n    }\n\n    fn collection_registered_in_namespace(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n        namespace: &Namespace,\n    ) -> Result<bool, AxonError> {\n        self.collection_exists_in_namespace(collection, namespace)\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Per-tenant PostgreSQL provisioning ───────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Validate a PostgreSQL database name to prevent SQL injection in DDL.\n///\n/// PostgreSQL identifiers may contain letters, digits, `_`, and `$`, and\n/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    if name.is_empty() {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\n            \"database name must not be empty\".to_owned(),\n        ));\n    }\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\"non-empty checked above\");\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() && first != '_' {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\"\n        )));\n    }\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\"\n        )));\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\n/// `dbname` key.\n///\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: &str, tenant_db_name: &str) -> String {\n    let target = format!(\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\");\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgres://\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgresql://\") {\n        // URL format: replace path component.\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\"://\") {\n            let after_scheme = &superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\n                let scheme_authority = &superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\n                let rest = &after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\n                    &rest[q..]\n                } else {\n                    \"\"\n                };\n                return format!(\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\");\n            }\n            // No path: append the target database.\n            return format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\");\n        }\n        format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\")\n    } else {\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\n        let mut parts: Vec<String> = superadmin_dsn\n            .split_whitespace()\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\"dbname=\"))\n            .map(str::to_owned)\n            .collect();\n        parts.push(format!(\"dbname={target}\"));\n        parts.join(\" \")\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\n/// superadmin connection.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\n///\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"CREATE DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if !exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"DROP DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\n// Explicit verification path:\n// AXON_TEST_POSTGRES=\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon_test\" cargo test -p axon-storage postgres::tests:: -- --nocapture\n// When `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` is unset, these tests attempt to provision PostgreSQL\n// via testcontainers and skip cleanly if the container runtime is unavailable.\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use std::{\n        ops::{Deref, DerefMut},\n        sync::{Mutex, MutexGuard, OnceLock},\n    };\n\n    use super::*;\n    use axon_core::types::Link;\n    use testcontainers_modules::postgres::Postgres as PgContainer;\n    use testcontainers_modules::testcontainers::{runners::SyncRunner, Container};\n\n    struct TestDatabase {\n        url: String,\n        _container: Option<Container<PgContainer>>,\n    }\n\n    enum TestSetupError {\n        Skip(String),\n        Fail(AxonError),\n    }\n\n    impl TestDatabase {\n        fn connect() -> Result<Self, TestSetupError> {\n            if let Ok(url) = std::env::var(\"AXON_TEST_POSTGRES\") {\n                return Ok(Self {\n                    url,\n                    _container: None,\n                });\n            }\n\n            let container = PgContainer::default()\n                .with_db_name(\"axon_test\")\n                .with_user(\"postgres\")\n                .with_password(\"postgres\")\n                .start()\n                .map_err(|error| {\n                    TestSetupError::Skip(format!(\n                        \"AXON_TEST_POSTGRES is unset and PostgreSQL test container startup failed: {error}\"\n                    ))\n                })?;\n            let host = container.get_host().map_err(|error| {\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\n                    \"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container host: {error}\"\n                )))\n            })?;\n            let port = container.get_host_port_ipv4(5432).map_err(|error| {\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\n                    \"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container port: {error}\"\n                )))\n            })?;\n\n            Ok(Self {\n                url: format!(\n                    \"host={host} port={port} user=postgres password=postgres dbname=axon_test\"\n                ),\n                _container: Some(container),\n            })\n        }\n\n        fn url(&self) -> &str {\n            &self.url\n        }\n    }\n\n    struct TestStore {\n        adapter: PostgresStorageAdapter,\n        _database: TestDatabase,\n    }\n\n    impl Deref for TestStore {\n        type Target = PostgresStorageAdapter;\n\n        fn deref(&self) -> &Self::Target {\n            &self.adapter\n        }\n    }\n\n    impl DerefMut for TestStore {\n        fn deref_mut(&mut self) -> &mut Self::Target {\n            &mut self.adapter\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn postgres_test_guard() -> MutexGuard<'static, ()> {\n        static GUARD: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();\n        GUARD\n            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))\n            .lock()\n            .expect(\"PostgreSQL test guard lock should not be poisoned\")\n    }\n\n    fn skip_postgres_test(test_name: &str, reason: &str) {\n        tracing::warn!(test = test_name, reason, \"skipping PostgreSQL storage test\");\n    }\n\n    fn database_or_skip(test_name: &str) -> Option<TestDatabase> {\n        match TestDatabase::connect() {\n            Ok(database) => Some(database),\n            Err(TestSetupError::Skip(reason)) => {\n                skip_postgres_test(test_name, &reason);\n                None\n            }\n            Err(TestSetupError::Fail(error)) => {\n                panic!(\"PostgreSQL test setup should succeed: {error}\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn store() -> Result<TestStore, TestSetupError> {\n        let database = TestDatabase::connect()?;\n        let adapter =\n            PostgresStorageAdapter::connect(database.url()).map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        // Clean tables for a fresh test.\n        adapter\n            .block(adapter.client.batch_execute(\n                \"TRUNCATE entities, schemas, collection_views, collections, namespaces, databases, audit_log RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE\",\n            ))\n            .map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        adapter\n            .ensure_default_namespace()\n            .map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        Ok(TestStore {\n            adapter,\n            _database: database,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn store_or_skip(test_name: &str) -> Option<TestStore> {\n        match store() {\n            Ok(store) => Some(store),\n            Err(TestSetupError::Skip(reason)) => {\n                skip_postgres_test(test_name, &reason);\n                None\n            }\n            Err(TestSetupError::Fail(error)) => {\n                panic!(\"PostgreSQL test setup should succeed: {error}\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn register_unique_namespaced_collection(\n        store: &mut TestStore,\n        qualified: &CollectionId,\n    ) -> (Namespace, CollectionId) {\n        let (namespace, bare_collection) = Namespace::parse(qualified.as_str());\n        let bare_collection = CollectionId::new(bare_collection);\n\n        store\n            .create_database(namespace.database.as_str())\n            .expect(\"database create should succeed\");\n        store\n            .create_namespace(&namespace)\n            .expect(\"namespace create should succeed\");\n        store\n            .register_collection_in_namespace(&bare_collection, &namespace)\n            .expect(\"collection register should succeed\");\n\n tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"test runtime\");\n        let (client, connection) = rt\n            .block_on(tokio_postgres::connect(url, NoTls))\n            .expect(\"test connection\");\n        rt.spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        (client, rt)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn postgres_roundtrip_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(mut s) = store_or_skip(\"postgres_roundtrip_when_available\") else {\n            return;\n        };\n\n        let col = CollectionId::new(\"tasks\");\n        let entity = Entity::new(\n            col.clone(),\n            EntityId::new(\"t-001\"),\n            serde_json::json!({\"title\": \"hello\"}),\n        );\n        s.put(entity).expect(\"test operation should succeed\");\n        let got = s\n            .get(&col, &EntityId::new(\"t-001\"))\n            .expect(\"test operation should succeed\")\n            .expect(\"test operation should succeed\");\n        assert_eq!(got.data[\"title\"], \"hello\");\n        assert_eq!(got.version, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn unregister_collection_cleans_up_legacy_collection_views_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(database) = database_or_skip(\n            \"unregister_collection_cleans_up_legacy_collection_views_when_available\",\n        ) else {\n            return;\n        };\n\n        let (legacy_client, legacy_rt) = connect_raw_for_test(database.url());\n        legacy_rt\n            .block_on(async {\n                legacy_client\n                    .batch_execute(\n                        \"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS collection_views;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS collections;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS namespaces;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS databases;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS entities;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS schemas;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS audit_log;\",\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                legacy_client\n                    .batch_execute(\n                        \"CREATE TABLE collections (\n                            name TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY\n                        );\n                        CREATE TABLE collection_views (\n                            collection    TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,\n                            version       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n                            view_json     JSONB NOT NULL,\n                            updated_at_ns BIGINT NOT NULL,\n                            updated_by    TEXT\n                        );\",\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                Ok::<_, tokio_postgres::Error>(())\n            })\n            .expect(\"legacy schema should be created\");\n\n        let collection = CollectionId::new(\"ephemeral\");\n        let legacy_view = CollectionView {\n            collection: collection.clone(),\n            description: Some(\"legacy view\".into()),\n            markdown_template: \"# {{title}}\".into(),\n            version: 1,\n            updated_at_ns: Some(42),\n            updated_by: Some(\"legacy\".into()),\n Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\n//! 9. TenantId, UserId};\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\n\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\npub struct JwtIssuer {\n    secret: Vec<u8>,\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\n    pub issuer_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl JwtIssuer {\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec<u8>, issuer_id: String) -> Self {\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\n    }\n\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\n    pub fn issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError> {\n        encode(\n            &Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\n            claims,\n            &EncodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret),\n        )\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n    }\n\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\n    ///\n    /// Result<JwtClaims, AuthError> {\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\n\n        decode::<JwtClaims>(token, &DecodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret), &validation)\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature => AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                _ => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n            })\n    }\n}\n\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\n///\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\n#[derive(Default)]\npub struct Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\n    pub fn contains(&self, jti: &Uuid) -> bool {\n        self.inner\n            .read()\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\n    pub fn insert(&self, jti: Uuid) {\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\n            g.insert(jti);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\n///\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\n///\n/// Install with:\n/// ```ignore\n/// ```\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\n    State(state): State<Arc<AuthPipelineState>>,\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(&path) else {\n        return next.run(request).await;\n    };\n\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\n    let database = database.to_string();\n    let method = request.method().clone();\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\n\n    match verify_request(&state, &headers, &method, &tenant, &database) {\n        Ok(identity) => {\n            let mut request = request;\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\n            next.run(request).await\n        }\n        Err(err) => AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nfn verify_request(\n    state: &AuthPipelineState,\n    headers: &HeaderMap,\n    method: &Method,\n    tenant: &str,\n    database: &str,\n) -> Result<ResolvedIdentity, AuthError> {\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\n\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\n    let claims = with 30 s skew.\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs();\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\n    // Expired: exp + skew < now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) < now {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\n    }\n    // Not yet valid: nbf > now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\n    if claims.nbf > now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\n    }\n\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\n        .jti\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(&jti_uuid) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n    }\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        if storage\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\n        {\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\n            drop(storage);\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\n    }\n\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\n    let user_id = UserId::new(&claims.sub);\n    let user = {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n    };\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\n    }\n\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\n    }\n\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\n    let granted_db = claims\n        .grants\n        .find_database(database)\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\n\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\n        Op::Read\n    } else {\n        Op::Write\n    };\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\n    }\n\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\n        user_id,\n        tenant_id,\n        grants: claims.grants,\n &HeaderMap) -> Result<&str, AuthError> {\n    let auth_header = headers\n        .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\n        .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n\n    let auth_str = auth_header\n (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\n        (Some(\"tenants\"), Some(tenant), Some(\"databases\"), Some(rest))\n            if !tenant.is_empty() && !rest.is_empty() =>\n        {\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\n            if database.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some((tenant, database))\n            }\n into_response(self) -> Response {\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\n        let body = json!({\n            \"error\": {\n                \"code\": self.0.error_code(),\n                \"message\": self.0.to_string()\n            }\n        });\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\n    .to_str()\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\n\n    auth_str\n        .strip_prefix(\"Bearer \")\n        .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n}\n\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\n///\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)> {\n    let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\n    let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\n    match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\n        (Some(\"tenants\"), Some(tenant), Some(\"databases\"), Some(rest))\n            if !tenant.is_empty() && !rest.is_empty() =>\n        {\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\n            if database.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some((tenant, database))\n            }\n        }\n        _ => None,\n    }\n}\n\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\n\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\n    fn into_response(self) -> Response {\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\n        let body = json!({\n            \"error\": {\n                \"code\": self.0.error_code(),\n                \"message\": self.0.to_string()\n            }\n        });\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n//!\n//! # Authentication\n//!\n//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n//!\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n//!\n//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n//!\n//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n//!\n//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n//!\n//! # Request flow\n//!\n//! ```text\n//! TCP accept\n//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\n//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n//!             └─ route handler\n//!                  ├─ extract Extension<Identity>\n//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n//!                  └─ handler logic\n//! ```\n//!\n//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\npub mod actor_scope;\npub mod auth;\nmod collection_listing;\npub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\npub mod control_plane;\npub mod control_plane_routes;\npub mod cors_config;\npub mod user_roles;\npub mod gateway;\npub mod idempotency;\npub(crate) mod mcp_http;\npub mod mcp_stdio;\npub mod rate_limit;\npub mod schema_registry;\npub mod serve;\npub mod service;\npub mod tenant_router;\n\npub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\npub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\npub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":43,"oldLines":6,"newStart":43,"newLines":7,"lines":[" //! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`]."," pub mod actor_scope;"," pub mod auth;","+pub mod auth_pipeline;"," mod collection_listing;"," pub(crate) mod embedded_ui;"," pub mod control_plane;"]},{"oldStart":60,"oldLines":5,"newStart":61,"newLines":6,"lines":[" pub mod tenant_router;"," "," pub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};","+pub use auth_pipeline::{InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};"," pub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;"," pub use service::AxonServiceImpl;"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01CK6wwgEq2n1LP4QVdfHsPL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01G11hf2mEssXRvin3Et23Gc","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs","content":"//! Unit tests for the JWT auth pipeline (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Covers:\n//! - Every row of the failure-mode table (14 variants)\n//! - JWT round-trip\n//! - ResolvedIdentity installation in request extensions\n//! - Revocation cache invalidation\n//! - Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\n            }],\n        },\n    };\n    let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n        &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n        &bad_claims,\n        &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n    )\n    .unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 3: Signature invalid → 401 credential_invalid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_signature_invalid() {\n    let wrong_issuer = axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n           databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01BcRLMXUs4TZUS5Hhv4m4p8","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01QqPQtyiL6uPht7wAo243yE","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs","content":"//! Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\n//!\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\nconst ISSUER: &str = \"integration-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"u-integration-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"integration-test-secret\";\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Integration User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\n    };\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"credential_wrong_tenant\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 database_not_granted ──────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"invoices\".to_string(), // URL says \"orders\"\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, grants);\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"database_not_granted\"\n    );\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":575,"cache_read_input_tokens":132304,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":575},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"cc1c03fa-01b8-4915-97d1-edc4d799b3f6"}

Embedded admin UI — assets from `ui/build` compiled into the binary.\n//!\n//! The `UiAssets` struct embeds every file under `ui/build` at compile time.\n//! The axum handler serves them at `/ui/*path` with correct `Content-Type`\n//! headers, and falls back to `index.html` for any unrecognised path so that\n//! SvelteKit client-side routing works correctly.\n//!\n//! **Build prerequisite**: `ui/build` must exist before `cargo build`.\n//! Run `cd ui && bun run build` (or `npm run build`) first.\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::{header, StatusCode, Uri};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse rust_embed::RustEmbed;\n\n/// All files under `ui/build`, embedded at compile time.\n///\n/// The `#[folder]` path is relative to the crate root (`crates/axon-server/`),\n/// so `../../ui/build` resolves to `ui/build` in the workspace root.\n#[derive(RustEmbed)]\n#[folder = \"../../ui/build\"]\nstruct UiAssets;\n\n/// Axum handler for `GET /ui` and `GET /ui/*path`.\n///\n/// Strips the `/ui` prefix, looks up the file in [`UiAssets`], and responds\n/// with the correct `Content-Type`.  Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\npub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n    let raw = uri.path();\n    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n    let asset_path = raw\n        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n        .unwrap_or(raw);\n    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n        \"index.html\"\n    } else {\n        asset_path\n    };\n\n    serve_asset(asset_path)\n}\n\nfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n        Some(content) => {\n            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n            Response::builder()\n                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n        }\n        None => {\n            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\n                None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":66,"startLine":1,"totalLines":66}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\n\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\n\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\n\n## When to Use\n\n- After implementing a tracked issue\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\n\n## What It Does\n\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\n2. **Format** commit message:\n   - First line: `<issue-id> <concise summary>` (under 72 chars)\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\n\n## Commit Message Format\n\n```\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\n\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\n\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\n```\n\n## Examples\n\n```bash\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z","isSynthetic":true}

# Solution Design: Agent Session Capture and Inspection

## Purpose

This design makes agent sessions inspectable end-to-end. The existing agent
runtime already tracks metadata; this tranche adds the prompt and response
bodies, correlation metadata, and compatibility rules needed for full session
review.

## Scope

- Capture full prompt and response bodies for agent invocations.
- Preserve harness, model, token, duration, and exit-code metadata.
- Attach workflow correlation metadata without coupling to HELIX internals.
- Keep old metadata-only session rows readable.
- Define the CLI and API inspection behavior.
- Define redaction and retention policy knobs.

## Storage Boundary

Agent session evidence is stored as bead-backed session records in a dedicated
collection, not in the primary active-work collection.

- A session may reference workflow identifiers such as `bead_id`, but the
  canonical prompt, response, and log bodies live in sidecar files referenced
  by the session record.
- The session metadata row uses the bead envelope plus agent-session fields in
  preserved extras.
- Bead evidence in the primary work queue may summarize a session, but it is
  not the authoritative session store.

## Data Model

### Session record

Session records are bead-backed metadata rows whose attachment fields point at
stored prompt, response, and log bodies.

Required fields:
- `id`
- `timestamp`
- `harness`
- `model`
- `prompt_len`
- `tokens`
- `duration_ms`
- `exit_code`
- references to stored prompt and response bodies

Optional fields:
- `error`
- references to stored stderr and stdout bodies
- `correlation`
- `redaction`
- `prompt_source`

### Correlation metadata

Correlation metadata is a free-form object used by workflow tools to attach
domain identifiers such as `bead_id`, `doc_id`, `workflow`, or `request_id`.
DDx stores the data but does not interpret workflow-specific keys.

## Inspection UX

- `ddx agent log` lists sessions in reverse chronological order.
- `ddx agent log <session-id>` renders the full stored prompt and response.
- The server's agent-session endpoints mirror the same data shape.

## Compatibility

- Existing metadata-only JSONL rows remain valid legacy session entries.
- Missing prompt, response, stderr, or correlation data is treated as absent
  evidence rather than a hard failure.
- The inspection path must not fail when it encounters older log rows during
  migration.

## Redaction and Retention

- Default retention is local and indefinite.
- Redaction is opt-in and config-driven.
- Redaction is applied before persistence so list/detail APIs never expose
  masked content accidentally.

## Behavior

1. The runner resolves the prompt and response for the invocation.
2. The runner records execution metadata, prompt length, token count, and duration.
3. The session writer stores one session row in the dedicated collection and
   writes any large bodies as sidecar files.
4. Session detail reads the stored row and referenced bodies.

All writes remain repository-local and read-only consumers do not mutate
session state.

ddx:
  id: API-002
  depends_on:
    - API-001
    - FEAT-008
    - SD-019
---
# API/Interface Contract: Epic Worker Surfaces

## Purpose

Define the server API, worker model, and UI surfaces for epic-scoped execution.
Epic workers differ from single-ticket workers: they own a persistent branch
and worktree, execute child beads sequentially, and land via a merge commit
rather than individual rebase+ff operations.

This contract extends the single-ticket supervisor (API-001) without modifying
it. The two worker modes coexist on the same server.

## Epic Worker Model

An epic worker is a long-lived worker that:

1. Claims one open epic bead
2. Creates a persistent epic branch: `epic/<bead-id>`
3. Creates a persistent worktree at `.ddx/.epic-wt-<bead-id>/`
4. Sequentially executes each execution-eligible child bead inside the epic worktree
5. After each child completes, commits the result on the epic branch
6. When all children are done (or the epic is explicitly finalized), merges the epic branch to the target branch with a regular merge commit
7. Cleans up the epic worktree and branch

### State Machine

```
CLAIMED → BRANCHING → EXECUTING_CHILDREN → MERGING → DONE
                ↑              │
                └── RETRY_CHILD (on child failure, continue with next)
```

- **CLAIMED**: Epic bead claimed, no branch yet
- **BRANCHING**: Creating epic branch and worktree
- **EXECUTING_CHILDREN**: Running child beads one at a time
- **RETRY_CHILD**: A child failed; record result and proceed to next child
- **MERGING**: All children done (or stopped), performing epic merge
- **DONE**: Epic merged or preserved, worker exits

### Child Execution

Each child bead executes as a normal `execute-bead` invocation inside the epic
worktree, with these differences:

- The base revision is the epic branch HEAD (not the project default branch)
- The child result is committed directly to the epic branch
- There is no individual land/preserve step — the child commit stays on the epic branch
- Failed children are recorded but do not block the next child
- The epic worker may be configured to stop on first child failure or continue

### Merge Gate

After all children are processed, the epic worker evaluates a merge gate:

1. Rebase the epic branch onto the current target branch HEAD
2. Run required post-epic checks (if any)
3. If clean: merge the epic branch to the target branch with `git merge --no-ff`
4. If conflicts: preserve the epic branch under `refs/ddx/epics/<bead-id>/` and report `land_conflict`

The merge commit message includes:
- Epic bead ID and title
- Number of children executed
- Number of children succeeded vs failed
- Worker ID and timestamp

## Server API

### Worker Endpoints

All worker endpoints are shared between single-ticket and epic workers. The
worker record includes a `mode` field to distinguish them.

#### `GET /api/agent/workers`

List all workers. Supports filtering:

| Query param | Values | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mode` | `single`, `epic` | Filter by worker mode |
| `state` | `running`, `exited` | Filter by state |
| `project` | `<project-id>` | Filter by project |

Response includes `mode` field on each worker record.

#### `GET /api/agent/workers/:id`

Show worker details. Epic workers include additional fields:

```json
{
  "mode": "epic",
  "epic_bead_id": "ddx-cf340665",
  "epic_branch": "epic/ddx-cf340665",
  "epic_worktree": ".ddx/.epic-wt-ddx-cf340665",
  "children_total": 5,
  "children_done": 3,
  "children_succeeded": 2,
  "children_failed": 1,
  "children_remaining": 2,
  "current_child": "ddx-abc123"
}
```

Single-ticket workers include `"mode": "single"` and omit the epic fields.

#### `POST /api/agent/workers/execute-epic`

Start an epic worker. Request body:

```json
{
  "epic_bead_id": "ddx-cf340665",
  "project": "ddx",
  "model": "z-ai/glm-5.1",
  "harness": "agent",
  "stop_on_failure": false
}
```

- `stop_on_failure`: if true, stop executing children after the first failure
  (default: false — continue with remaining children)

#### `POST /api/agent/workers/:id/stop`

Gracefully stop a running worker. For epic workers, stops after the current
child completes. Does not abort mid-execution.

### Queue Endpoints

#### `GET /api/beads/ready?mode=epic`

List execution-ready epic beads. An epic is execution-eligible when:

- Status is `open` or `in_progress`
- `execution-eligible` is not explicitly `false`
- Not in cooldown from a prior attempt
- Has at least one execution-eligible child bead

#### `GET /api/beads/:id/children`

List child beads of an epic. Response includes each child's execution status.

### Observability Endpoints

#### `GET /api/agent/workers/:id/children`

For epic workers, list child execution results:

```json
[
  {
    "bead_id": "ddx-abc123",
    "status": "success",
    "detail": "",
    "base_rev": "abc1234",
    "result_rev": "def5678",
    "attempt_id": "20260412T120000-abc"
  },
  {
    "bead_id": "ddx-def456",
    "status": "execution_failed",
    "detail": "iteration_limit",
    "base_rev": "def5678",
    "result_rev": "def5678",
    "attempt_id": "20260412T121500-def"
  }
]
```

#### `GET /api/agent/workers/:id/log`

Same as single-ticket worker log, but also includes child-level progress:

The schema now has the needed fields and mutations. I’m regenerating gqlgen output next, then I’ll fix any compile fallout from the new generated interfaces.
